message:
fix(pager): restore freed pages on early `?` returns in commit paths

The feddab17 fix correctly deferred freed-page promotion to Phase C,
but drained `self.freed_pages` into a local `pending_freed` Vec before
several fallible `?` operators. If any of those operations failed, the
function returned early and `pending_freed` was dropped — silently
losing freed pages forever (neither in self.freed_pages nor
inner.freelist).

Replace bare `?` operators between the drain and the commit_result
check with explicit match/if-let blocks that restore pending_freed
into self.freed_pages before propagating the error. Applied to all
six sites across commit() and commit_and_retain().

Also fix two secondary issues:
- serialize_freelist_to_write_set: remove mutation of inner.freelist
  during Phase A (normalize_freelist call leaked a side-effect visible
  to concurrent transactions even on commit failure)
- test assertion: change `||` to `&&` so freed_pages_reusable verifies
  BOTH allocated pages come from the freelist, not just one

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
